ALL SPECIALTIES / PHARMACY TECHNICIAN / NEW YORK

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

New York Pharmacy Technician Registration

New York Pharmacy Technician license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-03-18 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Pharmacy Technician applications in New York run through New York Board of Pharmacy. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Official Transcripts - Sent directly from your educational institution.
  • Exam Score Report - Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 4 to 6 Weeks
  • Fingerprinting Not required for most applicants.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in New York by submitting a new application to the New York Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any New York-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 4 to 6 Weeks.

  1. STEP 1

    Review Eligibility Requirements

    Visit the NYSED Office of the Professions Pharmacy Technician page (op.nysed.gov/professions/pharmacy-technician) to review all current eligibility criteria, including age, education, supervised experience, and examination requirements. Ensure you meet all prerequisites before proceeding.

    Fee: $175 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  2. STEP 2

    Download and Complete Application Form 1

    Download and accurately complete Form 1, 'Application for Licensure' for Pharmacy Technicians, from the NYSED Office of the Professions website. This form gathers your personal information and application details.

    Fee: $175 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  3. STEP 3

    Arrange for Supporting Documents (Forms 2 & 3)

    Arrange for your professional education program to complete and mail Form 2, 'Certification of Professional Education,' directly to NYSED. Similarly, arrange for your employer(s) to complete and mail Form 3, 'Certification of Supervised Experience,' directly to NYSED, if applicable, based on your pathway to licensure.

    Fee: $175 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  4. STEP 4

    Pass a Certification Examination

    Register for and pass one of the approved certification examinations: either the Pharmacy Technician Certification Exam (PTCE) administered by the Pharmacy Technician Certification Board (PTCB) or the ExCPT Exam administered by the National Healthcareer Association (NHA). Ensure your official scores are sent directly to the NYSED Office of the Professions.

    Fee: $175 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  5. STEP 5

    Submit Application Packet and Fee

    Mail your completed Form 1, along with a check or money order for the required licensure and first registration fee ($100), payable to the 'New York State Education Department,' to the mailing address for the NYSED Office of the Professions provided above.

    Fee: $175 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  6. STEP 6

    Monitor Application Status Online

    After submitting your application, create an account on My.NYSED.gov. This portal allows you to track the status of your application, review any deficiencies, and receive updates on your licensure process once your application has been received and processed by the department.

    Fee: $175 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$175
Background check
See board if applicable
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$175

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Board Contact

Board
New York Board of Pharmacy
Phone
518-474-3817, option 1, then extension 570
Website
op.nysed.gov/professions/registered-pharmacy-technicians/license-application-forms
Application page
Open application

Renewal

Pharmacy Technician renewal in New York follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Every 3 years
Renewal fee
$100
Continuing education
See board

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does a New York pharmacy technician license cost?

New York charges a $75 application fee plus a $100 first registration fee for registered pharmacy technicians. The triennial renewal fee is $100.

Is national certification required?

Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

Does New York have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?

No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in New York. To work in New York, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to New York?

The New York Office of the Professions asks applicants to allow six weeks before requesting a status update on a pharmacy technician application.

Can I start working in New York while my endorsement application is processing?

Contact the New York Board of Pharmacy to determine whether a temporary or trainee permit is available while your endorsement application is being processed. Requirements vary, and some applicants may need to wait for full approval before beginning work in New York.

At a glance

Endorsement fee

$175

Processing time

4 to 6 Weeks

Fingerprinting

Not required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Temporary license

Available · See board

National exam

See board

Compact

No · plan on endorsement

Renewal

Every 3 years · $100

Licensing board

New York Board of Pharmacy

op.nysed.gov/professions/registered-pharmacy-technicians/license-application-forms

518-474-3817, option 1, then extension 570

This page is maintained as a reference for pharmacy technicians and recruiters evaluating New York assignments.
